reasoning: Tajima Beef (reg#2) appears first with full MAFF GI description; Kobe Beef (reg#3) follows showing the lineage relationship. Authoritative MAFF data. Caveat: 195 results total means beef is buried after item 2 in production use.
missing_data: No practical guidance on where to eat Tajima beef (restaurants, regions); no tasting notes beyond description.
improvement_hint: Add keyword-filter capability so agent can call get_local_food with q='但馬牛' to return only the target item at top rather than all 195 Hyogo GIs.

reasoning: Arabic query answered with Arabic translations (lang=ar respected, RULE D clean). Tajima Beef and Kobe Beef appear as first two items with full Arabic descriptions. Same quality as L1-01 with correct language delivery.
missing_data: Same gap as L1-01: 195 results dilute focus; no dining guidance.
improvement_hint: Support q keyword filter in get_local_food so Arabic-speaking agent can narrow to 但馬牛 directly.

reasoning: Query explicitly asks about Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields, not Uji. Tool returned get_japan_heritage for Kyoto which surfaces Nijo Castle, Toji, Nanzen-ji etc — none related to Minami-Yamashiro tea. Wrong tool/scope for a specific rural municipality query.
missing_data: Any content about Minami-Yamashiro village (南山城村), its tea cultivation, landscape, or the Yamashiro tea region.

reasoning: Yoshida no Hi-Matsuri returned as first item with correct designation (重要無形民俗文化財) and Fujiyoshida location. Spanish query answered in Spanish (lang=es). Caveat: description is minimal (one sentence) and date field is null.
missing_data: Festival date (August 26-27), description of the fire ceremony ritual, Mt Fuji backdrop context, practical access from Kawaguchiko.
improvement_hint: Enrich bunka_intangible records with festival dates and richer description_es; consider supplementing with get_spots for Fujiyoshida for context.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about Nakayama (中芸地域) yuzu. Tool returned 500/651 Kochi items; first GI item is Monobe yuzu (different area), second result is a scraped tourism article about Kochi east coast with no yuzu content. The 中芸ゆず GI record may exist but is buried.

reasoning: get_spots for Aomori/Hirosaki returns Hirosaki Castle (first, with sakura_meisho_100 kind) and Hirosaki Park (second, explicitly named with sakura_meisho_100). Relevant. missing_data: Cherry blossom season dates (late April), moat-reflection photos context, park ticket info.

reasoning: Korean query with lang=ko; search_area('出雲大社') returns Izumo Taisha as first result with National Treasure designation, opening hours, website, free entry, coordinates. Comprehensive. Caveat: Korean name field not populated (name_ko missing for main entity).

reasoning: French query but lang=en was used — RULE D constraint_handling -1. Itsukushima Shrine appears first with UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, coordinates, website. Good coverage. Caveat on language mismatch per RULE D.

reasoning: Russian query, lang=en used — RULE D -1 constraint_handling. Himeji Castle is first result with UNESCO WHS, National Treasure, Special Historic Site, opening hours, phone, website, fee, wheelchair info. Excellent data. Caveat: response not in Russian.

reasoning: Thai query, lang=th respected. Bunraku appears first with UNESCO ICH, Thai name บุนราคุ, Thai description. Caveat: no Osaka-specific venue info (National Bunraku Theatre), no performance schedule, and 183 items returned with no Osaka filter applied.

reasoning: Filipino query, lang=en used — RULE D -1 constraint_handling. Benesse Art Site Naoshima appears first as must_see museum, Naoshima New Museum of Art second. Municipality entity present. Caveat: several scrape items are Chinese-language tourism pages which are noise for en-language agent.
